headline: Police use of force complaints rose 34% in 2024
context: IPCC received a 34% increase in complaints about excessive police use of force in 2024, totalling 892 formal complaints.

cards.big: 892 use-of-force complaints against police filed with IPCC in 2024
cards.sub: 34% increase over 2023 numbers amid calls for accountability reform

cards.big: IPCC Act 2022 limits commission to recommending disciplinary action only
cards.sub: No binding enforcement power over police despite oversight mandate

cards.big: 19 custodial deaths recorded by Suhakam in 2024 alone
cards.sub: Families receive minimal information about cause and circumstances of death

cards.big: Bandar Mahkota Cheras community documented 23 incidents in 6 months
cards.sub: Lower-income areas report disproportionately higher use of force

cards.big: 892 complaints with no binding outcomes means oversight without accountability
Inspector General of Police pledged body camera rollout for 5,000 officers by 2026
